what_it_does: Wraps Resemble AI's Chatterbox TTS as an HTTP service on port 4124, exposing a synth(text, voice, speed) function and a voices() listing through a shared voice.engines._service harness. It lazily loads ChatterboxTTS.from_pretrained, generates speech from a reference audio clip, and returns WAV bytes.
observations: The script removes its own directory from sys.path to avoid shadowing the installed chatterbox pip package.
Configuration is driven by environment variables: COMPANY_TTS_DEVICE, COMPANY_VOICE_REF, COMPANY_CHATTERBOX_EXAGGERATION, COMPANY_CHATTERBOX_CFG.
A reference clip is mandatory and the file fails loudly if it is missing or unset.
The speed parameter is accepted per a shared contract but intentionally not applied because Chatterbox has no speed control.
Generated audio is a torch tensor converted to WAV bytes via a shared helper using model.sr.
voices() returns the configured reference clip as the only selectable voice.
standouts: Explicit sys.path manipulation to resolve a file-name/package-name collision.
Fail-loud validation for the required reference clip.
Exposes Chatterbox's emotion-exaggeration knob through an environment variable.
conventions: Follows the shared engine-service pattern: serve(name, port, synth, voices, warm).
Uses lazy global singleton for the TTS model.
Uses type hints and from __future__ import annotations.
Prefers environment variables over hardcoded runtime configuration.
concerns: Mutating sys.path can be fragile and may hide other path issues.
Global mutable _model state with no thread-safety or reload mechanism.
No range validation for EXAGGERATION, CFG_WEIGHT, or speed.
voices() returns a placeholder string when COMPANY_VOICE_REF is unset, which may be passed as a voice identifier.
Assumes model.sr exists without guarding the attribute access.
notes: A Chatterbox TTS adapter whose most important detail is removing its own directory from sys.path so the installed chatterbox package is imported instead of this file.
questions: What exact interface does voice.engines._service.serve expect for synth and voices?
How is the separate Chatterbox venv created and invoked in production?
What is the refined-Australian reference clip and where is it stored?
Is ChatterboxTTS.from_pretrained safe to call during warm-up without a reference clip?
What does wav_bytes_from_array do with tensor dtype/shape normalization?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to run Chatterbox-TTS as an HTTP service mirroring tts_service.py, and the file does exactly that while adding Chatterbox-specific reference-clip and exaggeration handling.
intention_signals: Integrate Chatterbox as one voice-engine worker in a fleet of TTS services, making its unique emotion-exaggeration control available while preventing the common pitfall of a local file shadowing the pip package.
novelty: The deliberate sys.path scrubbing because the file name collides with the chatterbox package name, plus first-class exposure of Chatterbox's exaggeration parameter.
contribution: Provides a Chatterbox-specific adapter that turns text plus a reference clip into WAV audio and registers it with the shared engine service, preserving Chatterbox's cfg_weight and exaggeration settings as configurable parameters.
summary_for_embedding: voice/engines/chatterbox.py is a Chatterbox-TTS HTTP service adapter that lazily loads the model, requires a reference audio clip, and returns synthesized WAV bytes via a shared engine harness.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to plug Chatterbox's reference-clip voice cloning, with its emotion-exaggeration control, into the company's shared TTS engine service infrastructure.

symbols.does: Lazily loads and returns the singleton ChatterboxTTS model, scrubbing sys.path first to avoid package shadowing.
symbols.description: _engine is a lazy initializer that imports ChatterboxTTS only on first call, removes the script's own directory from sys.path so the installed pip package wins, and caches the loaded model in a global variable for reuse across synthesis requests.

symbols.does: Synthesizes WAV audio from text using a reference clip, with cfg_weight and exaggeration from environment variables.
symbols.description: synth validates that a reference clip is provided and exists, loads the model via _engine, calls ChatterboxTTS.generate with the reference clip and configured parameters, and converts the returned torch tensor into WAV bytes using the model's sample rate.

symbols.does: Returns the configured reference clip as the only available voice.
symbols.description: voices exposes Chatterbox's reference-clip-driven nature by returning a single-element list containing the configured COMPANY_VOICE_REF path, or a placeholder string telling operators to set the variable.
